title: Are there any multi-modal brower or desktop assistants I can discuss technical analysis technical analysis on charts?
author: Emotional_Honey_8338
selftext:
I’m looking for a desktop or browser multimodal AI assistant that can "see" my screen or desktop and act as a soundboard for technical analysis.

I don't need a bot that just highlights patterns (standard indicators already do that). I need a multimodal assistant I can actually debate with. I want to ask things like:

\- Argue against my bullish thesis here, what am I missing?  
\-The indicators say 'Buy,' but does the price action look exhausted?  
\-Does this volume profile look like a trap?

Maybe it would even ask to pull up another indicator. 

Basically a "Second Brain" to challenge my own bias, not just an arrow on a chart or autotrade - definitely not that?

replies.author: Emotional_Honey_8338
replies.body: Thanks. Any experience w those two? For TS all I saw were automated insights, but didn’t see chat functionality for brainstorming. For CB any suggestions on which LLM to plug into for TA?

comments.author: aimechatron7
comments.body:
TS has a side kick using Claude 4.5 / I get 25 messages per month - it’s good because it has all the data / indicators you can imagine - between the LLM access I have separately and sidekick it’s the lowest effort jet start. 

I am using opus desktop app, it is very good, avoid api it will drain you pretty fast. Using GPT as auditor, anti- hallucination agent.

comments.author: MannerSpecialist9464
comments.body: You should try Reseek. It's an AI second brain that organizes all your research and content with semantic search. While it doesn't analyze charts directly, its ability to manage and cross reference your notes, saved articles, and data could help you build that critical thinking framework you're looking for
